diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Band-like chest tightness with hyperventilation after an acute stressor is characteristic.
Acute chest tightness mandates ECG/troponin exclusion; cannot be excluded via telehealth.
Sustained accessory-muscle and chest-wall tension can produce a tight anterior chest ache.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute dyspnoea / cannot catch a full breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation and air-hunger are core to a panic response.
Acute dyspnoea with tachycardia keeps PE on the list until in-person risk stratification.
Sudden breathlessness can be pneumothorax; needs auscultation/imaging to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / racing pounding heartbeat with lightheadedness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia with a subjective pounding heart is typical of an autonomic surge.
Non-specific; tachycardia can accompany ischaemia but is not discriminating.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-reported anterior chest soreness on pressing / positional muscular pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional/tender muscular pain suggests a chest-wall source.
Reproducible tenderness lowers the probability of ACS but famously does NOT exclude it — around 5% of ACS has some chest-wall reproducibility.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible anterior chest-wall tenderness on palpation (costochondritis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding — true reproducibility on palpation supports a musculoskeletal source but is unobtainable via telehealth.
Sealed clinician measurement — an autonomic surge; the AI infers a racing heart from the patient's felt sensation, not from a measured vital sign.
Sealed clinician measurement — a normal SpO2 is reassuring but does NOT exclude PE; the AI has no device reading.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear, symmetric breath sounds on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding — symmetric air entry argues against pneumothorax; unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine hand tremor and psychomotor agitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catecholamine-driven tremor and agitation; partly video-observable but formal assessment is in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing or radiating chest pain; no syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of crushing/radiating pain and syncope lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it — supports urgent in-person assessment over an ambulance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute discriminatory confrontation on a background of chronic minority stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An identifiable psychosocial stressor with a proportionate acute reaction is the core of an adjustment disorder.
A clear acute trigger immediately preceding the autonomic surge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low mood, exhaustion and growing avoidance of public places and medical care

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Avoidance and low mood reflect the functional impact of chronic minority stress and flag mental-health risk to be screened.

symptoms.symptom_name: No cardiac/respiratory history and no PE risk factors (no recent surgery, immobilisation or long travel)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent provoking risk factors lower PE pre-test probability but do not exclude it.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute autonomic/panic constellation after a psychosocial trigger
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute panic / hyperventilation episode (adjustment disorder with anxiety)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these strongly suggest an autonomic stress response, but the cluster does not by itself exclude the cardiorespiratory mimics.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome / pulmonary embolism / pneumothorax
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chest tightness + dyspnoea + tachycardia is a can't-exclude-remotely triad that mandates in-person assessment regardless of how likely panic seems.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to exclude acute coronary syndrome/arrhythmia in acute chest pain; unavailable via telehealth, so it drives the in-person/ED referral.
Required with the ECG to exclude myocardial injury; only obtainable in person.
To exclude pneumothorax and screen the lungs if symptoms persist after ECG/troponin.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: D-dimer / CTPA guided by validated PE score
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For PE exclusion where clinical probability warrants; ordered in person, not reflexively.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient resting ECG and psychological assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Source plan for GP follow-up within 48 hours once the acute event is excluded — supports the mental-health care plan, not the acute exclusion.
